title: run,performance: check build stage status in `truncated exponential backoff` manner
body:
Buildah constantly polls running build container for it status every `0.1` second, this polling nature wastes CPU cycles unnecessarily so instead of checking status every `0.1` second use exponentially increasing counter.

Exponential counter has a max limit of `2 seconds` and delay will reset to certain milliseconds and will grow again to `2 seconds` this process will be repeated till status is returned from the build container.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
[NO TESTS NEEDED]
This is not a new feature but improvement to buildah so it stops consuming and producing unnecessary overhead for jobs with longer duration.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4481

this will make the build process slower, since it takes longer to realize the container terminated.

`crun state` is really fast, I don't see how it is a problem to run it 10 times per second

> this will make the build process slower, since it takes longer to realize the container terminated.
> 
> `crun state` is really fast, I don't see how it is a problem to run it 10 times per second

I think in original issue the concern was made for longer steps where build container is definitely gonna take time and even then buildah somehow keeps polling in background , hogging the resource via (exec calls). But still waiting for reply from @paravz on original issue https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4481 though maybe he is facing different issue than this one.

> I think in original issue the concern was made for longer steps where build container is definitely gonna take time and even then buildah somehow keeps polling in background , hogging the resource via (exec calls). But still waiting for reply from @paravz on original issue #4481 though maybe he is facing different issue than this one.

`crun state` is still cheap though.  I don't think it is a good idea to make the entire process slower just to reduce the number of fork/exec calls
Is there other ways we could check?  IE Watch for a exit code to appear?

> Is there other ways we could check? IE Watch for a exit code to appear?

@rhatdan No I think the actual issue was polling , but once actual problem is explained in detail and if it gets decided that its needed then maybe we can make exponential backoff configurable via a flag. So just waiting for discussion here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4481

> Is there other ways we could check? IE Watch for a exit code to appear?

we could poll directly the container process (even with pidfd where supported), so the fork/exec can be replaced with a kill() on older kernels, but I am not sure how well it would work with VMs like Kata
